reasoning: Based on the base rate of US recessions and current macroeconomic indicators, an imminent recession within the 2024-2025 window is unlikely. The US historically enters a recession approximately once every 6-10 years, which gives a base rate around 15-25% over a two-year period. By 2024, the economy demonstrated resilience with solid initial GDP prints, a strong labor market, and cooling inflation, allowing the Federal Reserve to transition toward an easing cycle to protect the expansion. Thus, absent unforeseen exogenous shocks, the probability of two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth starting between Q1 2024 and Q4 2025 is lower than historical peaks of concern, stabilizing around 15%.
